title: Slow Steamed Middle Eastern Lamb Kebabs
description: A pungent and crispy Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb, cocoa, and a lazy Sunday vibe, all while being paleo-friendly. This dish is perfect for a relaxing day at home.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit to create a warm and stable environment for steaming.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, cocoa powder, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to create the marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat, making sure each piece is fully covered.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers in a steamer basket, leaving enough space for the steam to circulate around each kebab.
Steam the kebabs over boiling water for 20 minutes, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the kebabs are steaming, prepare the mushimono-steam by boiling water in a large pot and reducing the heat to a simmer.
Once the kebabs are cooked, remove them from the steamer and place them on a plate to rest.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with chopped onion and a side of steamed vegetables if desired.
